name: Dreamoon Likes Sequences
statement:
Dreamoon likes sequences very much. So he created a problem about the sequence that you can't find in OEIS:
You are given two integers $d, m$, find the number of arrays $a$, satisfying the following constraints:
•  The length of $a$ is $n$, $n \ge 1$
•  $1 \le a_1  \lt  a_2  \lt  \dots  \lt  a_n \le d$
•  Define an array $b$ of length $n$ as follows: $b_1 = a_1$, $\forall i  \gt  1, b_i = b_{i - 1} \oplus a_i$, where $\oplus$ is the bitwise exclusive-or (xor). After constructing an array $b$, the constraint $b_1  \lt  b_2  \lt  \dots  \lt  b_{n - 1}  \lt  b_n$ should hold.
Since the number of possible arrays may be too large, you need to find the answer modulo $m$.
input:
The first line contains an integer $t$ ($1 \leq t \leq 100$) denoting the number of test cases in the input.
Each of the next $t$ lines contains two integers $d, m$ ($1 \leq d, m \leq 10^9$).
Note that $m$ is not necessary the prime!
output: For each test case, print the number of arrays $a$, satisfying all given constrains, modulo $m$.
